title: Public archive and local model triage after H045
scope: public web/lore search for the sent descriptor-fetch question

notes: Search found public U-Boot A733 support and older sunxi material, but no visible archive hit or reply for the manually sent A733 SDMMC0 IDMAC descriptor-fetch question.
The visible U-Boot A733 series remains a PIO/FEL boot-enablement source, not a Linux normal-IDMAC proof.

model_squeeze.summary: Strix Qwen3.6 Q6 produced speculative leads: hardware firewall, MBUS QoS, TrustZone handoff, cache timing, and clock phase. Cache timing, visible MBUS/NSI gates, and clock/register variants are already covered or too speculative. AMD returned an empty message. The dispatch route disconnected.

source_check: Local source searches found no named A733 SDMMC0/SMHC hardware firewall register, security handoff bit, or new MBUS master-permission register suitable for a behavior patch.
Vendor DTS/source still exposes only the known SDMMC0 clocks/properties: mmc_store, mmc_mbus, mmc_msi_lite, req-page-count, ctl-spec-caps, and v5p3x descriptor/threshold hooks.
The diagnostic Linux tree already has optional A733 mmc_mbus and mmc_msi_lite consumers plus prior fabric/NSI instrumentation.
reason: The only remaining plausible class is a hidden SDMMC0 firewall/security/master-permission handoff, but no source names a register or bit to test. Retesting cache, phase, QoS, or threshold variants would be guesswork against a pre-descriptor-fetch stall.
next: Continue monitoring for maintainer/list responses or public archive visibility.
Only promote a new runtime proof if a source or reply names a specific SDMMC0 master/firewall/security/interconnect bit, reset order, or firmware handoff.
